free_text: Neutron induced fission cross section ratios for 232Th,
235, 238U, 237Th and 239Pu from 1 to 400 MeV
WNR high intensity spallation neutron
source
A proton beam from the LAMPF incident on a
7.5 cm long, 3cm diameter tungsten target.
The spallation neutrons passed through 2.54 cm thick
polyethylene filter to reduce frame overlap.
Each sample in oxyde form with diameter of 10.2 cm was
vacuum evaporated onto 127 mum thick stainless steel
backing.
Fission foils deposits were typically 127 ug/cm2 thick.
Multiple plate gas ionization chamber located
at 20 m flight path
To measure incident neutron energy
Normalized to the results of Meadows at 1 - 10 MeV
No information on source of uncertainties
Data declared as preliminary data
Compiled by S.H.
On. Digitized data for 238U/235U (004),
See 14016.003-004 for data tabulated by authors.
On. MONITOR and MONIT-REF added.
